content.text:
Module:
Distributed Systems 1  |
Semester:
1  |
Type:
UEF12
VHS:
63h  |
Weekly hours:
4h30  |
Lectures:
1h30  |
TD:
1h30  |
TP:
1h30
Personal work VHS:
42h  |
Coefficient:
3  |
Credits:
4
Teaching Objectives
Introduce the fundamental principles of distributed systems
Understand communication models, classical problems and the basics of synchronization
Recommended Prerequisites
Algorithmics, operating systems and networks
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Chapter 1: Introduction to Distributed Systems
Definitions, objectives, expected properties
Application examples (Cloud, P2P, Blockchain, etc.)
Challenges of distributed systems
Middleware and software abstraction
Theoretical fundamentals: network, concurrency, scalability
Chapter 2: Distributed System Architectures
Taxonomies (hardware, OS, network, software)
Client-Server, Multi-tier, P2P, Proxy/Cache models
Microservices and containers (Docker, Kubernetes — intro)
Chapter 3: Distributed Communication Paradigms
Synchronous and asynchronous message passing
Remote Procedure Calls (RPC)
Remote Method Invocation (RMI)
Event-based and shared-memory communication
Group communication (basics)
Chapter 4: Time, Scheduling and Synchronization
Concurrency and causality
Physical time and synchronization (Cristian, Berkeley)
Logical clocks (Lamport, vector clocks)
Global and causal ordering
Application: Mutual exclusion (centralized and distributed)
Chapter 5: Fundamental Problems and Coordination
Naming and service discovery
Distributed coordination: deadlocks, synchronization, state management
Termination detection (Dijkstra's algorithm and related)
Assessment
Continuous assessment: 40%
Exam: 60%
Bibliographic References
G. Coulouris, J. Dollimore, and T. Kindberg, Distributed Systems - Concepts and Design, 3rd ed., Addison-Wesley, 2003.
A. S. Tanenbaum, M. van Steen, Distributed Systems - Principles and Paradigms, Prentice Hall, 2002.
Michel Raynal, Synchronisation et état global dans les systèmes répartis et Gestion des données réparties, Éditions Eyrolles, 1992.
M. Singhal, N. G. Shivaratri, Advanced Concepts in Operating Systems, McGraw-Hill, 1994.
Nancy Lynch, Distributed Algorithms, Elsevier, 1996.
Ajay D. Kshemkalyani, Mukesh Singhal, Distributed Computing: Principles, Algorithms, and Systems, Cambridge University Press, 2008.
Nicola Santoro, Design and Analysis of Distributed Algorithms, John Wiley & Sons, 2007.
Alexandre Duret-Lutz, Algorithmique Distribuée, Notes de cours.
Cyril Gavoille, Algorithmes distribués, Master 1&2, Université de Bordeaux, 2015.
Jie Wu, Distributed System Design, CRC Press LLC, 1999.
